div.p: Alexander by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know that we have granted and by this our charter made firm the final agreement made between the aboot and convent of Holyrood in Edinburgh on the one side and Ingram de Balliol on the other, over the marches of the lands of the churches of the same abbot and convent, that is, at Kirkconstantine and at Kirkbride, for the said abbot and convent to hold and to have of the aforesaid Ingram and his heirs by the correct bounds described in Ingram’s own charter and with all their revenues and pertinents, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably as the charter of the same Ingram de Balliol drawn up for the said abbot and convent the more fully declares. Saving our service. As witnesses, Master Matthew, chancellor, William Comyn, earl of Buchan, justiciar of Scotland, Ingram de Balliol, John de la Haye, Walter Comyn, David Comyn, William de Munfichet [Muschet], Alexander of Seton, Philip de Malville, sheriff of Aberdeen, David of Lindsay. At Aberdeen on the eighth day of July, in the fourteenth year of our reign.
